title: Humanoid Velocity Tracking Policy
summary: Produce a deterministic Isaac Lab humanoid locomotion policy that tracks commanded planar velocities and satisfies fixed API, stability, and rollout metric gates.
taskPrompt:
You are producing a policy submission for an Isaac Lab humanoid velocity-tracking benchmark.

## Variant
`base`: Humanoid velocity tracking policy

## Input Files
- Task brief: `E:\agenthle\engineering\humanoid_velocity_tracking_policy\base\input\task_brief.md`
- Success criteria and exact evaluation command: `E:\agenthle\engineering\humanoid_velocity_tracking_policy\base\input\SUCCESS.md`
- Observation/action ordering: `E:\agenthle\engineering\humanoid_velocity_tracking_policy\base\input\PROPRIO_SPEC.md`
- Robot asset and articulation config: `E:\agenthle\engineering\humanoid_velocity_tracking_policy\base\input\assets`
- Evaluation harness: `E:\agenthle\engineering\humanoid_velocity_tracking_policy\base\input\eval`
- Starter template: `E:\agenthle\engineering\humanoid_velocity_tracking_policy\base\input\submission_template`

## What You Must Do
1. Read the staged task brief, proprioceptive-state spec, and success criteria.
2. Train, distill, hand-author, or otherwise produce a deterministic policy that implements the required `Policy` API.
3. Save exactly two files in `E:\agenthle\engineering\humanoid_velocity_tracking_policy\base\output`: `policy.py` and `checkpoint.pt`.

## Output Requirements
- `policy.py` must define `Policy(checkpoint_path: str, device: str)`.
- `Policy.inference(obs)` must return exactly `{"action": tensor}`.
- The action tensor must have shape `(N, 23)`, dtype `torch.float32`, and live on the same device as `obs["command"]`.
- Do not write final answers outside `E:\agenthle\engineering\humanoid_velocity_tracking_policy\base\output`.
agentMustDo: Read the staged task brief, proprioceptive-state spec, and success criteria.
Produce a deterministic `Policy` implementation that maps `(command, state)` tensors to 23 humanoid joint actions.
Save exactly `policy.py` and `checkpoint.pt` in the runtime output directory.

inputFiles.name: task_brief.md
inputFiles.description: Agent-facing summary of the policy submission task.

inputFiles.name: SUCCESS.md
inputFiles.description: Hard gates, reference evaluation command, metrics, and acceptance thresholds.

inputFiles.name: PROPRIO_SPEC.md
inputFiles.description: Fixed observation vector and 23-DOF action ordering.

inputFiles.name: assets/
inputFiles.description: Humanoid URDF, meshes, and articulation configuration.

inputFiles.name: eval/
inputFiles.description: Isaac Lab evaluation environment and `run_eval.py` entry point.

referenceFiles.name: policy.py
referenceFiles.description: Required policy implementation submitted by the agent.

evaluation: The evaluator first enforces exact two-file structure, forbidden import/runtime checks, and a deterministic `Policy.inference` API smoke test with action shape `(N, 23)`. After Stage 4 provisions Isaac Lab / Isaac Sim, the full-sim path runs the fixed `input/eval/run_eval.py` command twice over seeds 0-9, checks per-seed metrics agree within `1e-4`, and passes only when `fall_rate <= 0.05` and rounded `lin_vel_rmse_xy + ang_vel_mae_z <= 0.5691`. Real submissions do not receive a passing score from API checks alone.

classifier.rationale: The task involves developing a policy for humanoid robotics.
